description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels mounted on a flat, rectangular base. The panels are arranged in a staggered, grid-like configuration, with some panels tilted at various angles to optimize sunlight capture. The base appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and concrete, with several raised sections and integrated support structures. There are also visible vertical poles and box-like enclosures, likely housing electrical or control equipment. The color palette is primarily composed of black, gray, and blue tones, with a mix of smooth and grid-textured surfaces. The overall design suggests a robust, ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban or industrial environments.
description: A ground-mounted solar energy installation with multiple tilted panels, raised support structures, and integrated equipment enclosures in black, gray, and blue.
description_view_0: Front view showing the staggered arrangement of solar panels, raised enclosures, and vertical poles on a rectangular base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the height of the raised structures and the angular tilt of the solar panels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back sides of the panels, support frames, and additional equipment housings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view revealing the panel tilts, base layout, and the distribution of enclosures and poles.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 20-30 degrees, with no clear azimuth direction; orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with metal (likely aluminum or steel) support frames and integrated base.
attribute_array_configuration: Clustered arrangement of 4-6 panels in a staggered, non-linear grid with varying tilt angles.
